source: Curated from Big 4 (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG) HK graduate programme pages, McKinsey/Bain/BCG HK entry requirements, HKIHRM reports, Glassdoor HK, CTgoodjobs, and talent.gov.hk (2025-2026)

roles.growthOutlook: Strong demand in HK driven by digital transformation, post-merger integration, and ESG consulting. Competitive entry with case interviews. MBB and Big 4 run structured graduate programmes annually.

requiredSkills.description: Break down complex business problems into structured frameworks (MECE). Apply hypothesis-driven approach to analysis. Core consulting skill tested in case interviews.
Build financial models and quantitative analyses in Excel; create client-ready presentations and storylines in PowerPoint. Daily tools for all consultants.
Analyze business data, build business cases, perform market sizing, and derive insights from quantitative analysis.
Present findings to C-level clients, facilitate workshops, manage difficult conversations, and build trusted advisor relationships.
Read and interpret financial statements, build basic financial models, and understand key business metrics and drivers.
Increasingly expected in data-driven consulting. Ability to query databases and build dashboards for client deliverables.
Manage workstreams within larger engagements, track project timelines, and coordinate cross-functional teams.
Fluent English essential for client deliverables, presentations, and communication in all major consulting firms in HK.
Important for local client engagements and government consulting projects. Less critical at MBB for international projects.
Increasingly valued for cross-border projects and serving mainland Chinese clients expanding internationally.

roles.growthOutlook: Big 4 (PwC, Deloitte, EY, KPMG) hire hundreds of audit graduates annually in HK. Structured career path with professional qualification support (HKICPA/ACCA). High turnover creates consistent demand. Peak season (Jan-Mar) involves long hours.

requiredSkills.description: Understanding of Hong Kong Financial Reporting Standards and International Financial Reporting Standards. Tested in professional exams and used daily.
Manipulate large datasets, perform analytical procedures, and prepare audit working papers. Pivot tables and lookup functions essential.
Understanding of audit planning, risk assessment, substantive testing, and sampling methodologies. Knowledge of professional standards (HKSA/ISA).
Analyze client financial statements, identify unusual trends, and assess going concern assumptions.
Review financial records, identify discrepancies, and ensure accuracy in audit documentation. Zero tolerance for errors in professional services.
Prepare audit reports, management letters, and use audit management software (CaseWare, Teammate, or firm-specific tools).
Work in audit teams of 3-10 members under tight deadlines. Support senior associates and communicate progress clearly.
Basic understanding of HK profits tax, salaries tax, and tax compliance. Some audit roles involve tax provision work.
Essential for audit documentation, professional correspondence, and working with international clients and IFRS standards.
Required for communication with local HK clients, understanding Chinese-language financial records, and internal team communication.
Useful for audit engagements with mainland Chinese subsidiaries and working with PRC-based colleagues.

roles.growthOutlook: HKEX mandatory ESG reporting driving rapid demand growth. HK government's green finance agenda and carbon neutrality targets creating new roles. Relatively new field with less experienced competition — good entry opportunity for graduates.

requiredSkills.description: Understanding of major ESG reporting frameworks and standards. Knowledge of HKEX ESG reporting guide and ISSB standards.
Collect, organize, and analyze ESG data (carbon emissions, diversity metrics, supply chain sustainability). Build ESG scorecards.
Engage with internal departments to gather ESG data, conduct stakeholder surveys, and present ESG performance to management and investors.
Understanding of Scope 1/2/3 emissions, carbon footprint calculation methodologies, and net-zero target setting.
Knowledge of HKEX ESG reporting requirements, SFC climate risk management guidelines, and HKMA green and sustainable banking principles.
Draft ESG reports, sustainability disclosures, and client deliverables. Clear, concise writing for investor and regulatory audiences.
Automate ESG data collection and analysis. Increasingly valuable as ESG data management scales up.
ESG reporting and investor communication is predominantly English-language. Essential for understanding global ESG frameworks.
Useful for stakeholder engagement with local HK companies and understanding China-specific ESG regulations.

roles.growthOutlook: Steady demand across all HK sectors. Growing specialization in HR analytics, employer branding, and talent development. Digital HR transformation creating new roles. Graduate programmes at major employers and HR consultancies.

requiredSkills.description: Understanding of HK labour laws, employment contracts, MPF regulations, and employee rights. Fundamental for any HK HR role.
Sourcing candidates, conducting interviews, assessment centre design, and managing the end-to-end hiring process.
Use HR information systems (SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, or local systems like IPL HRMS) for employee data management.
Analyze headcount data, turnover metrics, compensation benchmarks, and create HR reports for management.
Design training programmes, conduct learning needs analysis, and manage employee development initiatives.
Handle employee grievances, mediate conflicts, conduct exit interviews, and maintain positive workplace culture.
Understanding of salary benchmarking, bonus structures, benefits administration, and annual salary review processes.
Essential for communicating with local employees, handling employee relations matters, and understanding HK employment context.
Required for policy documentation, expatriate management, and communication in multinational organizations.
Increasingly useful for organizations with mainland Chinese operations, cross-border hiring, and working with PRC-based HR counterparts.
